narrative: As a white male applicant with a solid undergraduate GPA of 3.40 and an impressive LSAT score of 45—both above the typical student's marks of 3.21 and 36.70 respectively—your academic profile initially looks promising for law school admission. However, the university's prediction model, which assesses the likelihood of passing the bar exam on the first try based on students' past records, estimated your risk of failure at 60.8%. This probability tipped the scales toward rejection, as the school prioritizes applicants predicted to succeed on the bar to maintain their strong pass rates. The model's decision hinged most heavily on your family income bracket, which falls into the upper-middle range—a factor that, for applicants like you, stood out as the strongest push toward predicting bar failure, even though it's not uncommon. Next in influence was your Year 3 school decile ranking of 10, the lowest possible, which worked in your favor by pulling the prediction toward bar passage despite being well below the average of around 5.5. Your LSAT score rounded out the top influences, contributing toward the failure prediction, though its strength was moderated by how much higher it is than most applicants'. In comparison to typical students, your full-time enrollment plans align with the vast majority at 92.7%, and your Year 1 decile of 10 mirrors your later performance, but these didn't sway the outcome as much. Overall, while your test scores and GPA exceed averages, the model weighed your family background as the dominant concern, overriding some positives like your decile's unexpected benefit. This isn't a judgment on your potential as a white male student with your background—many similar applicants succeed—but the system's focus on bar passage risks led to this call. To move forward, consider retaking the LSAT if feasible to boost it further, or seek programs with holistic reviews that value your strengths beyond these predictors. Strengthening letters of recommendation or adding bar prep experience could also help reframe your profile for other schools. With targeted improvements, you're well-positioned to turn this around.
